name: Mewcha Amha Gebremedhin
title: Hydrologist | Remote Sensing Expert | Environmental Data Scientist
summary: Dr. Mewcha Gebremedhin is a hydrologist and remote sensing expert with extensive experience applying geospatial technologies to water resource assessments. His work integrates satellite-derived data with in-situ measurements to improve rainfall, evapotranspiration, and interception loss estimates, especially in data-scarce regions. His current research focuses on climate change impacts on surface-groundwater interactions, salinization in coastal areas, and nature-based solutions for resilient communities.
skills: Python, geospatial analysis, and scientific communication

education.degree: PhD in Geosciences, Hydrology and Water Resources
education.institution: University of Twente, Faculty of Geo-Information Science and Earth Observation, Netherlands

education.degree: MSc in Geoscience, Geo-Information and Earth Observation Science for Natural Resource Management
BSc, double major in Computer Science and Geoscience

experience.organization: Florida Gulf Coast University (FGCU), Florida, USA

Gebremedhin, M. A., Lubczynski, M. W., Maathuis, B. H. P., & Teka, D. (2022). Deriving potential evapotranspiration from satellite-based reference evapotranspiration, Upper Tekeze Basin, Northern Ethiopia. Journal of Hydrology: Regional Studies.

Gebremedhin, M. A., Maciek W. Lubczynski, Ben H. P. Maathuis, Teka T. (2020). Novel approach to integrate daily satellite rainfall with in-situ rainfall, Upper Tekeze Basin, Ethiopia. Atmospheric Research.

Gebremedhin, M. A. (2024). Integrating in-situ data with satellite-derived products to assess surface-groundwater interactions and sustainability of groundwater resources in semi-arid environment. PhD dissertation.

projects.name: Groundwater and Climate Resiliency
projects.description: Uses hydrological modeling and climate scenarios to understand future surface-groundwater interaction and salinity in coastal areas.

projects.name: Satellite Rainfall and Evapotranspiration
projects.description: Integrates satellite products with field measurements to improve water balance estimates in data-scarce basins.

projects.name: Machine Learning for South Florida Water Quality
projects.description: Applies machine learning models to understand nutrient and pesticide dynamics from agricultural fields to lakes.
